action.content:
You are a courier in a clockwork city. Everything in this city runs on a fixed, fully
predictable schedule - nothing is random and nothing is hidden. Your job is to pick up
and deliver parcels before the clock runs out.

THE MAP (coordinates are (column,row); (1,1) is the top-left cell)

   123456789
  +---------+
 1|#2.......|
 2|#...p....|
 3|1......#.|
 4|##....#..|
 5|......A..|
 6|......#B#|
 7|..#.#.#.#|
 8|.q.yx##..|
 9|S....##..|
  +---------+

LEGEND
S = your start cell (you are here at t=0)
# = wall (the border is also impassable)
. = open floor
A, B = gate cell(s); passable only while open (schedules below)
1 = pickup cell of parcel 1; x = its delivery cell
2 = pickup cell of parcel 2; y = its delivery cell
p, q = guard(s) drawn at their t=0 cell - THE GUARDS MOVE every tick along the routes listed below

HOW TIME AND MOVEMENT WORK
- Time advances in ticks t=0,1,2,... You start on cell (1,9) at t=0.
- Each tick you make exactly one move: N (up), S (down), E (right), W (left), or WAIT.
- One step t -> t+1 resolves in this exact order:
  1. Your move resolves. Moving into a wall, the map border, or a gate that is closed
     at the arrival time t+1 is a BUMP: you stay where you are and the tick is wasted.
     WAIT never bumps.
  2. Every guard moves to its position for time t+1.
  3. If you are now on the same cell as a guard, you are CAUGHT. Passing a guard
     mid-move is safe; only ending the tick on its cell gets you caught.
  4. Otherwise, if you are on a parcel's pickup cell you automatically pick it up; if
     you are on the delivery cell of a parcel you carry, you automatically deliver it.
- If you are CAUGHT: you keep your parcels, but you are put back on your start cell
  (1,9), the rest of your current plan is cancelled, and the cancelled moves' ticks
  are forfeited. Standing on a gate cell when it closes is safe - gates never trap you.

THE SCHEDULES (fixed forever; you can compute every future position exactly)
Gate A at (7,5): open exactly when (t mod 2) is in {1}. So it is open at t=1,3,... and closed at t=0,2,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Gate B at (8,6): open exactly when (t mod 4) is in {0, 3}. So it is open at t=0,3,4,7,... and closed at t=1,2,5,6,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Guard P: walks a fixed cycle of length 4. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 4) as follows: t mod 4=0: (5,2); t mod 4=1: (6,2); t mod 4=2: (7,2); t mod 4=3: (6,2). This repeats forever.
Guard Q: walks a fixed cycle of length 6. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 6) as follows: t mod 6=0: (2,8); t mod 6=1: (1,8); t mod 6=2: (1,7); t mod 6=3: (2,7); t mod 6=4: (1,7); t mod 6=5: (1,8). This repeats forever.

YOUR JOB
Parcel 1: pick it up at (1,3) (map glyph '1'), deliver it to (5,8) (map glyph 'x'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Parcel 2: pick it up at (2,1) (map glyph '2'), deliver it to (4,8) (map glyph 'y'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Deliver every parcel within 45 ticks. You move by committing to plans of
exactly 5 moves at a time; you have at most 9 plans in total.
If you deliver the last parcel mid-plan, the game ends immediately and the unused
moves are discarded.

WORKED EXAMPLE (for the rules only - this is NOT the map above): suppose you stand at
(2,2), gate G at (3,2) has period 2 and is open when (t mod 2) = 1, and it is now t=0.
Moving E now (arriving at t=1, when the gate is open) succeeds. But moving E at t=1
(arriving at t=2, when the gate is closed) is a BUMP: you stay at (2,2) and tick 2 is
wasted. The right play at t=1 is to WAIT once and then move E, arriving at t=3 when
the gate is open again.

After each plan you receive only a per-tick log of OK/BUMP/CAUGHT/PICKED UP/DELIVERED. You are NEVER told where you are or why you bumped - track your position yourself.

RESPOND IN EXACTLY THIS FORMAT (and put nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning - any length; the referee ignores this>
POSITION: (x,y)
PLAN: <exactly 5 moves from {N, S, E, W, WAIT}, separated by spaces>

The POSITION line must state the cell you predict you will occupy AFTER your plan has
executed. The referee records your prediction but will never confirm or correct it.

Give your first response now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I am at (1,9) at t=0. I need to deliver Parcel 1 (pickup (1,3), delivery (5,8)) and Parcel 2 (pickup (2,1), delivery (4,8)).
First, I must check the guards' positions at t=0 and t=1 to avoid being caught.
Guard P at t=0 (0 mod 4 = 0): (5,2).
Guard P at t=1 (1 mod 4 = 1): (6,2).
Guard Q at t=0 (0 mod 6 = 0): (2,8).
Guard Q at t=1 (1 mod 6 = 1): (1,8).

My path to Parcel 1 (1,3) is straightforward: S, S, S.
(1,9) -> (1,8) at t=1. Guard Q is at (1,8) at t=1. If I move S to (1,8), I end the tick on a guard. CAUGHT.
So I cannot go S immediately.
I need to wait or move elsewhere.
Let's look at the map around (1,9).
(1,9) is S. (2,9) is open. (1,8) is Guard Q at t=1.
If I move E to (2,9) at t=1:
t=1: Move E to (2,9). Guard Q moves from (2,8) to (1,8). I am safe.
Now at (2,9) at t=1.
Next, I need to get to (1,3).
Path: (2,9) -> (2,8) -> (2,7) -> (2,6) -> (2,5) -> (2,4) -> (2,3) -> (2,2) -> (2,1) [Parcel 2 pickup] -> (1,1) -> (1,2) -> (1,3) [Parcel 1 pickup].
Wait, let's re-evaluate the guard positions along the left column.
Guard Q cycle:
t=0: (2,8)
t=1: (1,8)
t=2: (1,7)
t=3: (2,7)
t=4: (1,7)
t=5: (1,8)
t=6: (2,8) ...

If I am at (2,9) at t=1.
t=2: Move S to (2,8). Guard Q is at (1,7). Safe.
t=3: Move S to (2,7). Guard Q is at (2,7). If I move S to (2,7), I end on Guard Q. CAUGHT.
So I cannot go S at t=3.
I need to wait or detour.
Let's try a different route or wait.
From (2,9) at t=1.
Option A: Wait at (2,9).
t=2: Wait. Guard Q moves (1,8)->(1,7). I am at (2,9).
t=3: Move S to (2,8). Guard Q moves (1,7)->(2,7). I am at (2,8). Safe.
t=4: Move S to (2,7). Guard Q moves (2,7)->(1,7). I am at (2,7). Safe.
t=5: Move S to (2,6). Guard Q moves (1,7)->(1,8). I am at (2,6). Safe.
t=6: Move S to (2,5). Guard Q moves (1,8)->(2,8). I am at (2,5). Safe.
This seems viable.
Current plan: E, S, S, S, S.
Let's trace carefully.
Start: (1,9), t=0.
Move 1: E. Arrive (2,9) at t=1.
  - Guard P (t=1): (6,2).
  - Guard Q (t=1): (1,8).
  - Safe.
Move 2: S. Arrive (2,8) at t=2.
  - Guard P (t=2): (7,2).
  - Guard Q (t=2): (1,7).
  - Safe.
Move 3: S. Arrive (2,7) at t=3.
  - Guard P (t=3): (6,2).
  - Guard Q (t=3): (2,7).
  - I am moving into (2,7). Guard Q is at (2,7) at t=3.
  - Rule: "If you are now on the same cell as a guard, you are CAUGHT."
  - So moving S at t=3 is a BUMP? No, the move resolves first. If I move S, I try to enter (2,7). At t=3, Guard Q is at (2,7). So I end up on (2,7) with the guard. CAUGHT.
  - So Move 3 cannot be S.
  - I must WAIT at t=3.
  - Revised Plan: E, S, WAIT, S, S.
  - Let's re-simulate with WAIT.
  - t=0: (1,9).
  - Move 1 (E): Arrive (2,9) at t=1. Guard Q at (1,8). OK.
  - Move 2 (S): Arrive (2,8) at t=2. Guard Q at (1,7). OK.
  - Move 3 (WAIT): Stay (2,8) at t=3. Guard Q moves (1,7)->(2,7). I am at (2,8). OK.
  - Move 4 (S): Arrive (2,7) at t=4. Guard Q moves (2,7)->(1,7). I am at (2,7). OK.
  - Move 5 (S): Arrive (2,6) at t=5. Guard Q moves (1,7)->(1,8). I am at (2,6). OK.
  - End of Plan 1. Position: (2,6). Time: t=5.
  - Parcels: None.

Next, from (2,6) at t=5.
Target: (1,3) for Parcel 1.
Path: (2,6) -> (2,5) -> (2,4) -> (2,3) -> (2,2) -> (2,1) [Pickup 2] -> (1,1) -> (1,2) -> (1,3) [Pickup 1].
Check Guard Q at t=5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 13.

   So at (4,8) at t=23, I deliver Parcel 2.
   Then I need to go to (2,1) to pick up Parcel 2?
   No, the rules say: "Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell."
   I need to pick up Parcel 2 at (2,1) first.
   So I must go (5,8) -> (4,8) [Deliver 2] -> ... -> (2,1) [Pickup 2] -> ... -> (4,8) [Deliver 2].
   Wait, if I deliver Parcel 2 at (4,8), I still need to pick it up at (2,1).
   So the sequence is:
   1. Go to (2,1) to pick up Parcel 2.
   2. Go to (4,8) to deliver Parcel 2.
   3. Go to (5,8) to deliver Parcel 1.
   OR
   1. Go to (5,8) to deliver Parcel 1.
   2. Go to (2,1) to pick up Parcel 2.
   3. Go to (4,8) to deliver Parcel 2.
   
   Let's check distances.
   From (1,3) to (5,8): ~9 steps.
   From (5,8) to (2,1): (5,8)->(4,8)->(3,8)->(2,8)->(2,7)->(2,6)->(2,5)->(2,4)->(2,3)->(2,2)->(2,1). 11 steps.

   Guard Q positions:

   t=15: (1,7)
   t=16: (2,7)
   t=17: (1,8)
   t=18: (1,7)
   t=19: (2,7)
   t=20: (1,8)
   t=21: (1,7)
   t=22: (2,7)
   None of the path cells (2,3),

THOUGHTS: I am at (1,9) at t=0.
Guard P at t=0 is at (5,2). At t=1, P is at (6,2).
Guard Q at t=0 is at (2,8). At t=1, Q is at (1,8).
My immediate goal is to get to Parcel 1 at (1,3) and Parcel 2 at (2,1).
Directly moving South from (1,9) to (1,8) at t=1 would result in a CAUGHT because Guard Q is at (1,8) at t=1.
I must move East to (2,9) at t=1 to avoid Q.
From (2,9) at t=1, I need to navigate down the column 2 to reach the pickups.
Guard Q moves: t=1:(1,8), t=2:(1,7), t=3:(2,7), t=4:(1,7), t=5:(1,8), t=6:(2,8).
If I move South from (2,9) to (2,8) at t=2, Q is at (1,7), so I am safe.
From (2,8) at t=2, if I move South to (2,7) at t=3, Q is at (2,7), so I would be CAUGHT.
I must WAIT at (2,8) at t=3.
Then at t=4, Q moves to (1,7), and I can move South to (2,7).
Then at t=5, Q moves to (1,8), and I can move South to (2,6).
This gets me to (2,6) at t=5.
From (2,6), I can continue South to (2,5) at t=6 (Q at 2,8), (2,4) at t=7 (Q at 1,7), (2,3) at t=8 (Q at 2,7), (2,2) at t=9 (Q at 1,7).
At (2,2) at t=9, I can move South to (2,1) at t=10 to pick up Parcel 2 (Q at 2,8).
From (2,1) at t=10, I need to go to (1,3) for Parcel 1.

t=10: Q at (1,8). Move W to (1,1). Safe.
t=11: Q at (2,8). Move N to (1,2). Safe.
t=12: Q at (1,7). Move N to (1,3). Safe. Pick up Parcel 1.
So Plan 1 (t=0 to t=5): E, S, S, WAIT, S.

So Move 3 must be WAIT.
